title: Red Braised Pork Belly with Jasmine Rice, Shandong Style
description: A traditional Shandong dish showcasing tender pork belly in a rich, fermented soybean paste sauce, served with fragrant jasmine rice.
instructions: Rinse the pork belly and pat it dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix together Doubanjiang, fermented soybean paste, rice vinegar, sesame oil, garlic, and ginger.
Rub the spice mixture all over the pork belly, making sure to coat it evenly.
Place the pork belly in a large Dutch oven or heavy pot and add 2 cups of water.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the pork for 2.5 hours, or until it's tender and easily shreds with a fork.
While the pork is braising, cook the jasmine rice according to package instructions.
Once the pork is done, remove it from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes.
Slice the pork into thin strips and serve it with the cooked jasmine rice and a sprinkle of scallions.
